channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenyan Environmentalist Truphena Muthoni to Attempt World Record for Most Trees Hugged in One Hour
item.description: Truphena Muthoni, a 22-year-old Kenyan environmentalist, is gearing up for another remarkable challenge in Brazil. She is aiming to set a new Guinness World Record by hugging 1,172 trees within a sing...

item.title: China Reports Strong Trade Growth in Early 2026 with Rising Exports and Imports
item.description: China's foreign trade started 2026 with strong momentum, showing double-digit growth in the total value of merchandise trade. Between January and February, the total trade value surged 18.3 percent ye...

item.title: Government to Review Planned September Fuel Duty Rise Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has announced that the planned rise in fuel duty scheduled for September will remain under review amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East. The rise, which would phase ...

item.title: Students Create AI-Generated Pages to Satirize School Teachers
item.description: A new trend among U.S. high school students involves the use of AI-generated content on social media to create 'slander pages' targeting teachers. Students are posting videos and memes that digitally ...

item.title: Paris Jackson disputes legal fees claimed by her father’s estate
item.description: Paris Jackson am just challenging the legal fees claimed by her late father Michael Jackson’s estate. The whole matter, wey don dey ongoing for Los Angeles courts, involve how the estate been dey mana...

item.title: Islamabad High Court imposes fine on NAB over delay in Al-Qadir Trust case against Imran Khan and Bushra Bibi
item.description: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) on Wednesday imposed a fine of Rs100,000 on NAB's special prosecutor due to deliberate delays in proceedings related to the £190 million Al-Qadir Trust corruption case i...

item.title: Von der Leyen acknowledges Europe’s nuclear phase-out was a strategic mistake
item.description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen recently admitted that Europe’s decision to reduce its reliance on nuclear power was a 'strategic mistake.' She highlighted that Europe, which genera...

item.title: Wike Upgrades Abuja Chiefs Following Area Council Election Support
item.description: Minister Nyesom Wike don approve upgrade for traditional rulers for Abuja after area council elections. E come as way of thank you because FCT people show strong support for Bola Tinubu and APC candid...

item.title: Converge dominates Macau as Mikey Williams marks PBA comeback
item.description: In a highly anticipated match, the Converge FiberXers delivered a strong performance against the Macau Black Knights, giving them a tough introduction to the game. The spotlight, however, was on Mikey...

item.title: Emfuleni Municipality Loses Millions in Undelivered Vehicle Deal
item.description: A forensic investigation has revealed that the Emfuleni Local Municipality lost R8.7 million after paying for six trucks that were never delivered, despite invoices being signed and stamped. The inves...

item.title: Coimbatore Police Introduce WhatsApp Chatbot for Citizen Services
item.description: The Coimbatore District Police have recently introduced an innovative digital service named 'Kovai Digi Cop', which is a WhatsApp-based chatbot designed to assist citizens with various police-related ...

item.title: Extra growth cycles in crocodiles challenge how scientists estimate dinosaur ages
item.description: Researchers from the University of Cape Town have discovered that Nile crocodiles can form several growth cycles within a single year, rather than just one annual cycle as previously assumed. This fin...

item.title: WA farmers face fuel shortages as metropolitan demand strains regional supply
item.description: Western Australian farmers are grappling with severe diesel shortages due to panic-buying in the metropolitan areas, with some facing up to three-week waits for new fuel supplies. This shortage threat...

item.title: Amit Shah Questions Rahul Gandhi's Attendance During Key Lok Sabha Sessions
item.description: In a detailed address to the Lok Sabha, Union Home Minister Amit Shah sharply criticised Congress leader Rahul Gandhi for low attendance during parliamentary sessions, particularly coinciding with imp...

item.title: Controversial Statue Depicting Donald Trump and Jeffrey Epstein Installed on National Mall
item.description: A provocative new statue depicting former U.S. President Donald Trump and the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as characters from the movie Titanic has been installed on the National Mall i...

item.title: City of London special constable and former Hong Kong police officer on trial for spying for China
item.description: A trial has begun at the Old Bailey in London involving two men accused of breaching the UK's National Security Act by assisting a foreign intelligence service linked to Hong Kong and China. Chi Leung...

item.title: Zanzibar government vows to restrict firearm use by Special Unit amid complaints
item.description: Zanzibar government has recognised public complaints about the Special Unit forces using violence and confiscating goods at Darajani Main Market. During a press briefing on the 100-day achievements of...

item.title: Philippines coordinates with Portugal for the extradition of Zaldy Co
item.description: The Philippine government, under President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., is actively coordinating with Portuguese authorities to facilitate the return of Zaldy Co. Manila has issued a red notice through Inter...

item.title: Analysis of US-Africa Health Funding Deals and Potential Risks
item.description: By February 2026, US don sign health funding deals worth nearly US$20 billion with 18 African countries, including Nigeria, Kenya, Ethiopia, and South Africa. US go provide US$12.2 billion, while Afri...

item.title: NADRA Updates Records by Cancelling CNICs of Deceased Citizens
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Nadra has taken a major step to ensure the accuracy of Pakistan’s national identity records by cancelling approximately 4.2 million CNICs of individuals who are recorded as deceased in prov...
